sessions.intent: Design test contract for purging System.IO from PLang — derivation verbs, .goal MIME, Goal/Path typing flip, AppGoals migration, action-handler denial paths, PLNG002 analyzer, and the .Absolute discipline rule.

before.assumptions: Architect plan v(final) approved by Ingi; 13 design decisions settled. Stage 1 is greenfield (no derivation verbs yet). Existing FileSystem/Surface/Permission tests give the canned-channel + root-setup pattern I'll reuse.
before.risk: Tests must define behaviour for code that doesn't yet compile (handlers still hold string Path). Signatures only — Assert.Fail bodies until coder implements.

actions.context: C# TUnit test signatures (Assert.Fail bodies) covering 11 batches mapped 1:1 onto the architect's stages 1–6. Reuses CannedChannel/StatelessChannel patterns from FileSystemPermissionFlowTests for actor + permission-prompt setup.
PLang .test.goal scaffolds (throw 'not implemented') for ring-2 handler denial paths. Follows the repo convention <Scenario>/Start.test.goal used by existing Tests/Permission/Authorize/ tests.

One deliberate divergence from architect: pulled D13 (.Absolute discipline) out of Stage 7 (docs) and gave it its own test batch with a mutation-test placeholder. The rule is only real if a test catches a missing Authorize.

after.notes: ~80 C# test signatures + 8 PLang .test.goal scaffolds written across 11 batches. All bodies are Assert.Fail/throw — coder bot picks up next, stage order 1→7 from architect's plan.
